name: Spicy Meat Pies with Yogurt Sauce
recipeInstructions:
- FILLING:
- Ground lamb or beef, 1/2 lb
- Chopped onion, 1/2 c
- Shredded carrot, 1/4 c
- Pine nuts or slivered almonds, 2 tb
- Fresh parsley, chopped, 2 tb
- Dried mint leaves, 1/2 tsp
- Dried oregano leaves, 1/4 tsp
- Salt, 1/4 tsp
- Pepper, 1/8 tsp
- Grated lemon peel, 1 ts
- Lemon juice, 1 tb
- Garlic clove, minced
- BISCUITS:
- Pillsbury Grands! or Flaky Biscuits, 1 can (refrigerated buttermilk or refrigerated)
- Sesame seed, 1 tsp
1. In a small bowl, combine sauce ingredients.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
3. Cook lamb and onion in a large skillet over medium heat until meat is no longer pink and onion is tender, stirring occasionally. Drain excess fat if necessary.
4. Add remaining filling ingredients; cook and stir for one minute.
5. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
6. Separate dough into 8 biscuits.
7. Roll each biscuit into a 5-inch circle.
8. Place about 1/4 cup of filling in the center of each biscuit.
9. Fold dough over filling to form a half-circle.
10. Press edges together, seal with a fork.
11. Place biscuits on an ungreased cookie sheet.
12. Sprinkle tops with sesame seeds and press lightly into biscuits.
13. Bake for 13 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
14. Remove from the oven and serve hot meat pies with yogurt sauce (1 cup).
